rulings.Case Title: SQUARE CAPITAL, LLC VS. SPREAD'Z CATERING GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Grant Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 13, 2019, line 1. PETITIONER SQUARE CAPITAL, LLC'S Motion To Grant Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Square Capital, LLC's petition to confirm the arbitration award is granted. No opposition filed. The Court will sign the proposed order and judgment submitted with the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: QIAN & NEMECEK LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. PETER M. RADIN JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application By Qian & Nemecek Llp For Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Peter M. Radin, Jr.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 13, 2019, Line 6, Application By Qian & Nemecek LLp For Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Peter M. Radin, Jr. Judge Pro Tem Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required, parties to appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KATHLEEN ORTIZ VS. MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Paga Settlement Approval; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 13, 2019, line 2. PLAINTIFFS KATHLEEN ORTIZ, JOANNA ORTIZ'S Motion For PAGA Settlement Approval. Plaintiffs Kathleen Ortiz and Joanna Ortiz's motion for PAGA settlement approval is granted. The Court finds that the overall amount of the proposed settlement is fair and adequate in view of the purposes and policies of PAGA, taking into account such factors as the adequacy of the civil penalties in light of the potential maximum if the matter were to go to verdict and the risks of litigation. (Lab. Code § 2699(l)(2).) The Court further finds that the amount of attorney's fees and costs sought is reasonable. (Lab. Code § 2699(g)(1).) The Court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DAWN ANN MASON ET AL VS. FCA US, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Compliance With (1) The Courts February 4, 2019 Discovery Order And (2) Prospective Sanctions Of $500 Per Day
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 13, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFFs DAWN MASON and JAMES MASON's Motion For An Order Compelling Compliance With (1) The Courts February 4, 2019 Discovery Order And (2) Prospective Sanctions Of $500 Per Day Judge Pro Tem Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel compliance is GRANTED, Defendant to serve documents and verified supplemental responses within seven (7) days. The request for sanctions is continued to May 30, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
